mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean during rate cuts over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which sustains corporate earnings momentum and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04 over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production signals supply-side bottlenecks and reduced manufacturing output, which compresses near-term corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of contraction episodes during post-shock recovery periods over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A narrowing spread between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates falling real interest rates that boost equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The real rate spread fails to predict 20-day returns with a statistically significant positive coefficient (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in total nonfarm payrolls reflect weakening economic activity that reduces aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show a positive or zero mean during payroll declines over a 3-year rolling test, with Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent signals robust economic expansion that supports near-term earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of high-growth quarters over a 5-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial output and rise in joblessness signals a deepening recessionary phase that forces equity risk premium repricing, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of divergence episodes during recessionary onset periods over a 10-year sample.
